name_romaji: Ikazuchi-jinja Bun'ei Jūichi-nen Mei Itabi (tsuketari Ikazuchi-jinja Denrai Itabi)
name_en: Itabi at Ikazuchi Shrine inscribed with the Bun'ei 11 (1274) date, with associated shrine itabi
description_en: An itabi (stone stupa tablet) at Ikazuchi Shrine bearing an inscription dated to the eleventh year of the Bun'ei era (1274), together with an associated itabi handed down at the shrine. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 2022-04-22. Located in the Higuchi area.

name_en: Itabi at Enmei-ji inscribed with the Bunna 3 (1354) date
description_en: An itabi at Enmei-ji temple bearing an inscription dated to the third year of the Bunna era (1354). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (archaeological material) by Chikusei City on 2022-04-22. Located at Enmei-ji temple.

name_romaji: Abe no Seimei Denki Hangi, Hachiman Inari Gonge-chō Denki Hangi
name_en: Printing woodblocks for biographies of Abe no Seimei and the Hachiman-Inari Gonge-chō
description_en: Printing woodblocks (hangi) used for biographical texts of Abe no Seimei and for the Hachiman-Inari Gonge-chō biography. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Chikusei City on 2000-03-24.

A kōsatsudai, a stand for displaying official public notice boards (kōsatsu). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (historical material) by Chikusei City on 2002-02-25. Located in the Sekimoto-bunchū area.

name_en: Register of karmic-bond devotees, with an Amida Nyorai image
description_en: A kechien kyōmyōchō, a register listing devotees who formed a karmic bond, accompanied by one figure of Amida Nyorai. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Higuchi area.
A sennin-butsu, a votive object bearing a thousand Buddhist images. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1978-04-01. Located in the Sekidate area.
A gusoku, a complete matching suit of samurai armor. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1978-01-25.

A sangaku, a votive tablet (ema) presenting mathematical problems in the tradition of the Seki school. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1979-03-01. Located in the Hachiman area.

A sangaku, a votive tablet (ema) presenting mathematical problems in the tradition of the Seki school. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1979-03-01. Located in the Kuwayama area.

name_romaji: Ninomiya Sontoku Shihō no Suisha to Masu
name_en: Waterwheel and measuring box from Ninomiya Sontoku's reform methods
description_en: A waterwheel and measuring box (masu) associated with the agricultural reform methods (shihō) of Ninomiya Sontoku. Municipally designated as a tangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1979-10-01. Located in the Hanada area.
A daidai kagura, a form of Shinto sacred music and dance. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1975-04-24. Performed in the Sekimotokami area.

The Tokiwa-ren, a folk performing group known for its hyottoko (comic mask) dance. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1976-04-01. Based in the Matsubara area.

name_en: Yudate hot-water festival of Ikazuchi Shrine
description_en: The yudate matsuri, a hot-water purification ritual held at Ikazuchi Shrine. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Held in the Higuchi area.

name_en: Fire-walking rite of the Tsuji settlement
description_en: The hiwatari, a fire-walking rite practiced by the Tsuji settlement. Municipally designated as an intangible folk cultural property by Chikusei City on 1984-03-19. Held in the Tsuji area.
A historic site comprising the mausoleum (byō) of Date Yukitomo. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Nakadate area.
The Ashimayama Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Tokumochi area.

name_en: Graves of the patriots of the Kabasan Incident
description_en: A historic site comprising the graves of the shishi (patriots) of the Kabasan Incident. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Otsu area.
The Ozakata Site, an archaeological site. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Ozakata area.
The Ozakata tumulus cluster, including the Shinmeizuka mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Ozakata area.
The Shimoezure Jūniten Site, an archaeological site. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Shimoezure area.

name_en: Graves of successive generations of the Mizunoya family
description_en: A historic site comprising the graves of successive generations of the Mizunoya family. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Okazeri area.
The ruins of Shimodate Castle. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Kō area.
The Nodono Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1976-06-28. Located in the Nodono area.

name_en: Memorial pagoda of Fujiwara no Takafusa (Tōgaku-in memorial pagoda)
description_en: A memorial pagoda (kuyōtō) dedicated to Fujiwara no Takafusa, also known as the Tōgaku-in memorial pagoda. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Izumi area.
The Nishikata Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nishikata area.
The Nishikata Shinbatake Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nishikata area.
The Yakushi Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Kō area.

name_romaji: Shimodate Hanshu Ishikawa Fusakan no Haka
name_en: Grave of Ishikawa Fusakan, lord of Shimodate domain
description_en: The grave of Ishikawa Fusakan, a lord of the Shimodate domain. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nakadate area.
The Fujihigashi Kofun, also called Sengenyama, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nakadate area.
The Fujihigashi Kofun, also called Tera-ushiroyama, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nakadate area.
The grave of Satō Hidenobu. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Okazeri area.
The Daibatake Kofun, an ancient burial mound. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 1985-12-24. Located in the Murata area.
The grave site of Iida Gunzō. Municipally designated as a monument (historic site) by Chikusei City on 2000-07-25. Located in the Kido area.
A great keyaki (Japanese zelkova) tree at Kannon-ji temple. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Nakadate area.

name_en: Great zelkova of the Yakushi Hall
description_en: A great keyaki (Japanese zelkova) tree at the Yakushi Hall. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Chikusei City on 1977-03-24. Located in the Kō area.
A keyaki (Japanese zelkova) tree at Sekimoto Shrine. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Chikusei City on 1979-10-01. Located in the Sekimotokami area.

name_en: Camellia of the former Iō-ji temple (Kanki-in, Gekkaizan)
description_en: A yabutsubaki (common camellia) associated with the defunct Iō-ji temple (Kanki-in, Gekkaizan). Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Chikusei City on 1993-10-21. Located in the Okazeri area.
A great yanagi (willow) tree at Ichinobe. Municipally designated as a monument (natural monument) by Chikusei City on 1999-03-24. Located in the Ichinobe area.
The sanmon (main gate) of Kannon-ji temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Ueto 624.
The haiden (worship hall) of Ōu Shrine. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1969-12-03. Located at Ōu 814.
